Telemetry.__comment: story-selection/04 (anonymous serve log). StorageConnectionString is supplied PER-ENVIRONMENT (Key Vault / an App Service app setting / an env var - never committed here). When it is empty or absent the API uses the NoOp sink and runs with zero Azure setup (AC-05); when it is present the API writes anonymous, PII-free 'template served' events to Azure Table Storage. NO secret ever lives in this file.

Accounts.__comment: accounts-identity/02 (lightweight purchaser account, #68). StorageConnectionString is supplied PER-ENVIRONMENT (Key Vault / an App Service app setting / an env var - never committed here). Empty or absent => the API uses the working in-memory account store and runs with zero Azure setup (so accounts-identity/03 sign-in / restore is testable locally); present => accounts persist to Azure Table Storage. TokenSigningKey is a SECRET (the magic-link HMAC signing key) and is NEVER committed here - supply it from user-secrets / env / a Key Vault-backed app setting only, and NEVER a VITE_* var (AC-06). Absent => a random ephemeral key is generated per process (tokens work within the process lifetime). NO secret ever lives in this file.

Entitlements.__comment: billing-entitlements/01 (purchaser entitlement grants, #70). StorageConnectionString is supplied PER-ENVIRONMENT (Key Vault / an App Service app setting / an env var - never committed here). Empty or absent => the API uses the working in-memory grant store and runs with zero Azure setup (so the session-creation gate and stories 03-05 are testable locally); present => grants persist to Azure Table Storage, partitioned by a hash of purchaser identity. It reuses the SAME storage account as Accounts/Telemetry - no new resource. The Stripe webhook idempotency ledger (billing-entitlements/03) reuses this SAME connection string. NO secret ever lives in this file.

Stripe.__comment: billing-entitlements/03 (Stripe integration, #72). SecretKey and WebhookSigningSecret are SECRETS - supplied PER-ENVIRONMENT from Key Vault / user-secrets / an env var, NEVER committed here and NEVER a VITE_* var (AC-01). Empty SecretKey => billing is OFF: the app registers the disabled no-op checkout service and the webhook returns 503, so it runs with ZERO Stripe setup. PublishableKey is NOT a secret (the browser gets its own copy via a VITE_* var); it is left blank here. PastDueGraceDays is the dunning grace window a past_due subscription's lease is extended by rather than expired (ADR 0002 Decision D). NO secret ever lives in this file.

Operator.__comment: sysadmin-console/01 (operator back office, #135). AllowedEmails is the SEPARATE operator allowlist - who may hold an operator session. It is supplied PER-ENVIRONMENT as an App Service application setting SOURCED FROM KEY VAULT - either as an indexed array (Operator__AllowedEmails__0, Operator__AllowedEmails__1, ...) or, since a KV secret is a single string whose name cannot carry the '__' array indexer, as a SINGLE delimited secret (Operator__AllowedEmails = 'a@x.com; b@y.com', split on ';' or ','); it is NEVER a VITE_* var (it must not ship to any browser), NEVER inferred from player / purchaser data, and is committed EMPTY here so a fresh clone / dev / CI has ZERO operators (fail-closed). Adding or removing an operator is a config change (edit this list / the app setting), NOT a code change. The allowlist is checked at magic-link VERIFY time, never at issue time - a valid link alone never grants operator scope (AC-02). NO secret ever lives in this file.

Ai.__comment: ai-cost-gate/01 (server-side AI proxy). Endpoint + Deployment are NON-secret and select the Foundry-backed client when Endpoint is present; empty/absent => the API uses the no-op AI client and runs with zero AI setup (AC-04). ApiKey is a SECRET and is NEVER committed here - supply it from user-secrets / env / a Key Vault-backed app setting only, and NEVER a VITE_* var (AC-03). Preferred auth is the App Service managed identity (no key). InputCostPerMillion / OutputCostPerMillion sit beside Deployment so a model swap is a one-place change; story 04's spend estimator reads them. ADR 0001 picked gpt-4o-mini but it (and the gpt-4o/gpt-4.1 mini family) is Deprecating with nano at 0 quota, so gpt-5-mini was deployed (0.25 / 2.00 per 1M) - see the ADR Update note.
